title: USD Inherits Namespace Source Contract

included: wrapping a base NamespaceSource with direct inherits composition
reading source-visible inheritPaths fields from base prim inputs
listOp<ObjectPath> explicit inherit paths, otherwise prepend followed by append
mapping inherited prim roots and descendants onto the inherit-authoring prim path
recursive direct inherit expansion through inherited source prims
implied inherit arcs from stronger upstream layer-stack sites at the same inherited source path
remapping path-valued field contents authored under inherited source specs into the inherit-authoring namespace
same-path local layer-stack target opinions stronger than inherited opinions
implied upstream inherited opinions stronger than weaker reference-introduced target opinions
inherits opinions stronger than variants, relocates, references, and payloads by LIVERPS strength placement
effective population specifier selection across local and inherited specs
ordered child prim name composition across local and inherited specs
ordered property name composition across local and inherited specs
diagnostics for invalid inherit paths and missing inherited prims
deferred: unbounded inherit recursion without cycle diagnostics
full listOp composition beyond the source-visible field value exposed by the base source
interleaved inherits and specializes expansion beyond the ordered providers in this graph
attribute value resolution and time sample interpolation
provider-owned stage population or instancing behavior

model.InheritsNamespaceSource: A namespace-source provider backed by a composed non-variant base NamespaceSource that applies direct inheritPaths arcs.
model.BaseNamespaceSource: The composed local/reference/relocates namespace input, currently produced by usd-relocates-namespace-source in this bounded graph. usd-composition-arbitrator is the terminal provider that can merge payload opinions with inherited opinions.
model.InheritArc: A source-visible object path item from a prim's inheritPaths field, identifying a prim whose composed opinions are inherited by the prim where the arc is authored.
model.InheritMapping: The inherited source prim path mapped to the inherit-authoring prim path, with descendants and properties projected by replacing the inherited source prefix with the target prim prefix.
model.RecursiveInheritClosure: The transitive closure of inherit arcs reachable through inherited source prims. A source prim's own opinions are stronger than opinions introduced by its inherited sources.
model.ImpliedInheritArc: An inherit arc implied at an upstream layer-stack site that contributes stronger opinions at the same inherited source path as a weaker authored inherit target.
model.ContributingSources: Adapter rendering of strong-to-weak ContributingOpinion records. Domain records store source identity separately from semantic spec paths.

input.baseSource: target-native NamespaceSource exposing inheritPaths and composed prim/property opinions

output.inherits: direct inherit arcs with primPath and sourcePath

output.exists: whether target-local or inherited source specs contribute a prim-like input at the path
output.childNames: composed child prim names before stage mask or active filtering
output.propertyNames: composed property names before schema fallback injection
output.contributingSources: strong-to-weak target-local or inherited source contributors

output.exists: whether target-local or inherited source specs contribute an attribute or relationship at the path
output.fields: strongest authored field per field key
output.contributingSources: strong-to-weak target-local or inherited source contributors

input.path: absolute composed prim path, variant-selection prim path, or /

output.opinions: strong-to-weak contributing opinions after direct inherits have been applied

laws: the inherits source consumes a base NamespaceSource and must not require stage population to know whether opinions came from base layers, references, payloads, relocates, or inherits
the inherits source must not open files, resolve assets, or inspect parser-local USDA syntax
inherits are read from the source-visible inheritPaths field on base-source prim inputs

inherited source resolution must be source-visible and layer-stack aware: a target path named by an inherit arc is resolved through the applicable composed base namespace or layer-stack context, not only through the layer where the inherit arc text was authored
inherit source records must retain inherit-authoring prim paths, inherited source paths, and mapped contributor paths as semantic path handles/references rather than raw strings
inherits contributingSources domain data must be typed ContributingOpinion records, not composite source:path strings
inherit path items must be prim paths and must not be property paths
a missing inherited prim must be diagnosed with the inherit-authoring prim path and inherited source path
the inherited source prim maps to the inherit-authoring prim path, and descendants are mapped by replacing the inherited source prefix with the inherit-authoring prim path
properties under an inherited source prim map to the corresponding property under the inherit-authoring prim path

direct inherit arcs authored on inherited source prims must be expanded transitively, preserving the rule that each source prim's own opinions are stronger than opinions introduced by that source prim's inherited arcs

recursive inherit expansion must detect cycles by semantic inherit-authoring path, inherited source path, and mapping context; cycles must be diagnosed and must not recurse unboundedly or crash generated implementations

when a referenced or otherwise weaker layer stack authors an inherit to a source path and stronger upstream layer stacks contribute specs at that same source path, implied inherit arcs must make those upstream source-path opinions available at the inherit-authoring prim

implied inherit opinions from stronger upstream layer-stack sites are stronger than weaker referenced or payload-introduced local opinions on the inheriting prim according to LIVERPS; an implementation must not lose them by resolving the inherit target only inside the referenced asset layer

path-valued field contents authored on inherited prims or properties, including listOp<ObjectPath> targetPaths and connectionPaths, must be remapped by replacing the inherited source prefix with the inherit-authoring prim path before the fields are emitted
inherited targetPaths and connectionPaths elements outside the inherited source subtree remain in the shared composed namespace because direct inherits map between prims in the same namespace
path-valued field remapping is namespace composition and does not imply full listOp composition, dictionary merging, or attribute value resolution
same-path local layer-stack target opinions are stronger than inherited opinions
reference-introduced or payload-introduced target fields must not be flattened ahead of stronger implied upstream inherited opinions
inherited opinions are applied at inherits LIVERPS strength: weaker than local opinions and stronger than variants, relocates, references, payloads, and specializes in the full composition model

the concrete graph may evaluate inherits after relocates so the provider can see source opinions available from earlier bounded providers; this evaluation order must not be read as field-strength order

specializes composition may consume the output inherits NamespaceSource when direct specializes are in scope; the terminal non-variant source then feeds variant composition so variant selections can observe inherited non-variant opinions before selected variants are inserted at their LIVERPS strength
nested inheritPaths authored on inherited source prims participate in recursive direct inherit expansion in this provider
implied inherit arcs from represented upstream layer-stack sites participate in this provider
for non-special field keys in this first contract, field maps expose the strongest authored field value and do not combine dictionaries, listOps, time samples, or values
childNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring child_ordering=composed
propertyNames returned by this source are already composition-ordered; stage population must preserve this order for sources declaring property_ordering=composed
adapter JSON may seed base, sublayer, reference, and relocate layers for conformance tests, but domain APIs exchange NamespaceSource, path, token, and field value objects

adapter_protocol.input: A JSON file containing one case with rootLayer, optional sublayers map/list, optional referenceLayers map, and an ops array. The adapter constructs the configured base namespace-source chain before constructing the inherits namespace source.
adapter_protocol.output: A JSON object with ok, diagnostics, and results.
